records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2176 - "And seven hundred Prophets have prayed in the Mosque of Al-Khaif at Mina."
records.arabicText: 2176 - وَ قَدْ صَلَّى فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلْخَيْفِ بِمِنًى سَبْعُمِائَةِ نَبِيٍّ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2333 - Abdul Malik ibn Utbah asked Abu Abdullah (as) about garments from the coverings of the Kaaba that reach us. He inquired whether it is permissible to wear any of it.
Imam (as) replied: "It is suitable for children, for covering copies of the Quran, and for use as cushions, seeking blessings through it, if Allah (swt), the Exalted, wills."
records.arabicText: 2333 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنُ عُتْبَةَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَمَّا يَصِلُ إِلَيْنَا مِنْ ثِيَابِ اَلْكَعْبَةِ هَلْ يَصْلُحُ لَنَا أَنْ نَلْبَسَ شَيْئاً مِنْهَا فَقَالَ «يَصْلُحُ لِلصِّبْيَانِ وَ اَلْمَصَاحِفِ وَ اَلْمِخَدَّةِ تَبْتَغِي بِذَلِكَ اَلْبَرَكَةَ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2333 - Abdul Malik ibn Utbah asked Abu Abdullah (as) about garments from the coverings of the Kaaba that reach us. He inquired whether it is permissible to wear any of it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "It is suitable for children, for covering copies of the Quran, and for use as cushions, seeking blessings through it, if Allah (swt), the Exalted, wills."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2177 - "The Mosque of the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, during his time, extended from the minaret in the middle of the mosque and beyond it toward the Qibla for about thirty cubits, as well as to its right, left, and behind it, approximately the same distance."
records.arabicText: 2177 - وَ كَانَ مَسْجِدُ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَلَى عَهْدِهِ عِنْدَ اَلْمَنَارَةِ اَلَّتِي فِي وَسَطِ اَلْمَسْجِدِ وَ فَوْقُهَا إِلَى اَلْقِبْلَةِ نَحْوَ ثَلاَثِينَ ذِرَاعاً وَ عَنْ يَمِينِهَا وَ عَنْ يَسَارِهَا وَ خَلْفِهَا نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Mosque of the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, during his time, extended from the minaret in the middle of the mosque and beyond it toward the Qibla for about thirty cubits, as well as to its right, left, and behind it, approximately the same distance."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2178 - Whoever prays one hundred Rak’ahs in the Mosque of Mina before leaving it will receive the reward equivalent to seventy years of worship.
Whoever glorifies Allah (swt) one hundred times in the Mosque of Mina, Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, will record for them the reward of freeing a slave.
Whoever declares the oneness of Allah (swt) (La ilaha illa Allah (swt)) one hundred times in it will have the reward of giving life to a soul.
And whoever praises Allah (swt) (Alhamdulillah) one hundred times in it will receive the reward equivalent to the revenue of the two Iraqs (al-'Iraqayn) spent in the way of Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic.
records.arabicText: 2178 - وَ مَنْ صَلَّى فِي مَسْجِدِ مِنًى مِائَةَ رَكْعَةٍ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ مِنْهُ عَدَلَتْ عِبَادَةَ سَبْعِينَ عَاماً وَ مَنْ سَبَّحَ اَللَّهَ فِي مَسْجِدِ مِنًى مِائَةَ تَسْبِيحَةٍ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ أَجْرَ عِتْقِ رَقَبَةٍ وَ مَنْ هَلَّلَ اَللَّهَ فِيهِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ عَدَلَتْ إِحْيَاءَ نَسَمَةٍ وَ مَنْ حَمِدَ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيهِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ عَدَلَتْ أَجْرَ خَرَاجِ اَلْعِرَاقَيْنِ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2334 - It has been narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "I took some dust from the Maqam and soil from the House, along with seven pebbles."
Imam (as) replied: "What you have done is not good. As for the dust and the pebbles, return them."
records.arabicText: 2334 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَخَذْتُ سُكّاً مِنْ سُكِّ اَلْمَقَامِ وَ تُرَاباً مِنْ تُرَابِ اَلْبَيْتِ وَ سَبْعَ حَصَيَاتٍ فَقَالَ «بِئْسَ مَا صَنَعْتَ أَمَّا اَلتُّرَابَ وَ اَلْحَصَى فَرُدَّهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2334 - It has been narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "I took some dust from the Maqam and soil from the House, along with seven pebbles."
Imam (as) replied: "What you have done is not good. As for the dust and the pebbles, return them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2179 - "The pilgrim, when they stand at Arafat, is cleansed of their sins."
records.arabicText: 2179 - وَ اَلْحَاجُّ إِذَا وَقَفَ بِعَرَفَاتٍ خَرَجَ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2335 - It has been narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Abdullah (as):
"It is not appropriate for anyone to take soil from around the House (Kaaba), and if someone takes anything from it, they must return it."
records.arabicText: 2335 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِأَحَدٍ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ مِنْ تُرْبَةِ مَا حَوْلَ اَلْبَيْتِ وَ إِنْ أَخَذَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْئاً رَدَّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2335 - It has been narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not appropriate for anyone to take soil from around the House (Kaaba), and if someone takes anything from it, they must return it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2180 - Abu Ja’far (as) said: "No one, whether righteous or sinful, stands on those mountains (at Arafat) without Allah (swt) answering their prayer. As for the righteous, their supplication is answered for both their Hereafter and their worldly life. As for the sinful, their supplication is answered for their worldly life."
records.arabicText: 2180 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَا يَقِفُ أَحَدٌ عَلَى تِلْكَ اَلْجِبَالِ بَرٌّ وَ لاَ فَاجِرٌ إِلاَّ اِسْتَجَابَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ فَأَمَّا اَلْبَرُّ فَيُسْتَجَابُ لَهُ فِي آخِرَتِهِ وَ دُنْيَاهُ وَ أَمَّا اَلْفَاجِرُ فَيُسْتَجَابُ لَهُ فِي دُنْيَاهُ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No one, whether righteous or sinful, stands on those mountains (at Arafat) without Allah (swt) answering their prayer. As for the righteous, their supplication is answered for both their Hereafter and their worldly life. As for the sinful, their supplication is answered for their worldly life."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2336 - Hudhayfah ibn Mansur said to Abu Abdullah (as):
"My uncle swept the Kaaba and took some of its soil, and we use it for treatment."
Imam (as) replied: "Return it to the Kaaba."
records.arabicText: 2336 - وَ قَالَ حُذَيْفَةُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : إِنَّ عَمِّي كَنَسَ اَلْكَعْبَةَ فَأَخَذَ مِنْ تُرَابِهَا فَنَحْنُ نَتَدَاوَى بِهِ فَقَالَ «رُدَّهُ إِلَيْهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2336 - Hudhayfah ibn Mansur said to Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"My uncle swept the Kaaba and took some of its soil, and we use it for treatment."
Imam (as) replied: "Return it to the Kaaba."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2181 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "There is no man from a region who stands at Arafat among the believers without Allah (swt) forgiving the believers of that region. And there is no man from a household who stands at Arafat among the believers without Allah (swt) forgiving the believers of that household."
records.arabicText: 2181 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَا مِنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ كُورَةٍ وَقَفَ بِعَرَفَةَ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لِأَهْلِ تِلْكَ اَلْكُورَةِ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ مَا مِنْ رَجُلٍ وَقَفَ بِعَرَفَةَ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لِأَهْلِ ذَلِكَ اَلْبَيْتِ مِنَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2181 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no man from a region who stands at Arafat among the believers without Allah (swt) forgiving the believers of that region. And there is no man from a household who stands at Arafat among the believers without Allah (swt) forgiving the believers of that household."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2337 - Zayd Al-Shahham said to him (Abu Abdullah (as)):
"May I take a pebble out of the mosque?"
Imam (as) replied: "Return it or place it in another mosque."
records.arabicText: 2337 - وَ قَالَ لَهُ زَيْدٌ اَلشَّحَّامُ : أُخْرِجُ مِنَ اَلْمَسْجِدِ حَصَاةً قَالَ «فَرُدَّهَا أَوِ اِطْرَحْهَا فِي مَسْجِدٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2337 - Zayd Al-Shahham said to him (Abu Abdullah (as)):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"May I take a pebble out of the mosque?"
Imam (as) replied: "Return it or place it in another mosque."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2338 - It has been narrated by Al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja‘far (as), who said: "It is not appropriate for a person to reside in Mecca for a full year."
I asked: "What should he do?"
Imam (as) replied: "He should move away from it. Also, it is not appropriate for any building to be raised higher than the Kaaba."
records.arabicText: 2338 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُقِيمَ بِمَكَّةَ سَنَةً» قُلْتُ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ «يَتَحَوَّلُ عَنْهَا وَ لاَ يَنْبَغِي أَنْ يُرْفَعَ بِنَاءٌ فَوْقَ اَلْكَعْبَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2338 - It has been narrated by Al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja‘far (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"It is not appropriate for a person to reside in Mecca for a full year."
I asked: "What should he do?"
Imam (as) replied: "He should move away from it. Also, it is not appropriate for any building to be raised higher than the Kaaba."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2182 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) heard a beggar asking people on the Day of Arafah. He said to him: "Woe to you! Are you asking anyone other than Allah (swt) on this day? Indeed, it is hoped that even what is in the wombs of pregnant women will be destined for happiness on this day."
records.arabicText: 2182 - وَ سَمِعَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : يَوْمَ عَرَفَةَ سَائِلاً يَسْأَلُ اَلنَّاسَ فَقَالَ لَهُ «وَيْحَكَ أَ غَيْرَ اَللَّهِ تَسْأَلُ فِي هَذَا اَلْيَوْمِ إِنَّهُ لَيُرْجَى لِمَا فِي بُطُونِ اَلْحَبَالَى فِي هَذَا اَلْيَوْمِ أَنْ يَكُونَ سَعِيداً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2182 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) heard a beggar asking people on the Day of Arafah. He said to him:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Woe to you! Are you asking anyone other than Allah (swt) on this day? Indeed, it is hoped that even what is in the wombs of pregnant women will be destined for happiness on this day."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2183 - Abu Ja’far (as) would never turn away a beggar on the Day of Arafah.
records.arabicText: 2183 - وَ كَانَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ عَرَفَةَ لَمْ يَرُدَّ سَائِلاً.
records.thaqalaynMatn: would never turn away a beggar on the Day of Arafah.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2339 - It has been narrated: "Residing in Mecca hardens the heart."
records.arabicText: 2339 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ اَلْمُقَامَ بِمَكَّةَ يُقْسِي اَلْقَلْبَ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2184 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"On the eve of Arafah, Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, sends two angels who examine the faces of the people. If they find a man who has accustomed himself to performing Hajj but is absent, one of them says to the other: 'O so-and-so, what happened to so-and-so?'
The other responds: 'Allah (swt) knows best.'
Then one of them says: 'O Allah (swt), if poverty has kept him from Hajj, enrich him. If debt has kept him, relieve him of his debt. If illness has kept him, cure him. And if death has kept him, forgive him and have mercy on him.'"
records.arabicText: 2184 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِذَا كَانَ عَشِيَّةُ عَرَفَةَ بَعَثَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مَلَكَيْنِ يَتَصَفَّحَانِ وُجُوهَ اَلنَّاسِ فَإِذَا فَقَدَا رَجُلاً قَدْ عَوَّدَ نَفْسَهُ اَلْحَجَّ قَالَ أَحَدُهُمَا لِصَاحِبِهِ يَا فُلاَنُ مَا فَعَلَ فُلاَنٌ قَالَ فَيَقُولُ اَللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ قَالَ فَيَقُولُ أَحَدُهُمَا اَللَّهُمَّ إِنْ كَانَ حَبَسَهُ عَنِ اَلْحَجِّ فَقْرٌ فَأَغْنِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ حَبَسَهُ دَيْنٌ فَاقْضِ عَنْهُ دَيْنَهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ حَبَسَهُ مَرَضٌ فَاشْفِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ حَبَسَهُ مَوْتٌ فَاغْفِرْ لَهُ وَ اِرْحَمْهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2184 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"On the eve of Arafah, Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, sends two angels who examine the faces of the people. If they find a man who has accustomed himself to performing Hajj but is absent, one of them says to the other: 'O so-and-so, what happened to so-and-so?'
The other responds: 'Allah (swt) knows best.'
Then one of them says: 'O Allah (swt), if poverty has kept him from Hajj, enrich him. If debt has kept him, relieve him of his debt. If illness has kept him, cure him. And if death has kept him, forgive him and have mercy on him.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2340 - It has been narrated by Dawud Al-Raqqi from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"When you have completed your rituals, then return, for it will make you more eager to come back."
records.arabicText: 2340 - وَ رَوَى دَاوُدُ اَلرَّقِّيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «إِذَا فَرَغْتَ مِنْ نُسُكِكَ فَارْجِعْ فَإِنَّهُ أَشْوَقُ لَكَ إِلَى اَلرُّجُوعِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2340 - It has been narrated by Dawud Al-Raqqi from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When you have completed your rituals, then return, for it will make you more eager to come back."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2185 - Imam (as) said: "When a person prays for their brother in their absence, a caller from the Throne announces, 'For you is one hundred thousand times the like of it.' But when they pray for themselves, they receive only one.
Therefore, one hundred thousand guaranteed is better than one uncertain, as it is not known whether their personal supplication will be answered or not."
records.arabicText: 2185 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِذَا دَعَا اَلرَّجُلُ لِأَخِيهِ بِظَهْرِ اَلْغَيْبِ نُودِيَ مِنَ اَلْعَرْشِ وَ لَكَ مِائَةُ أَلْفِ ضِعْفِ مِثْلِهِ وَ إِذَا دَعَا لِنَفْسِهِ كَانَتْ لَهُ وَاحِدَةٌ فَمِائَةُ أَلْفٍ مَضْمُونَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ وَاحِدَةٍ لاَ يُدْرَى يُسْتَجَابُ لَهُ أَمْ لاَ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2341 - It has been narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a tree whose root is in the non-sacred area (hill) and its branches extend into the sacred area (Haram).
Imam (as) replied: "The root is sanctified because of the branch's location."
I then asked: "What if its root is in the Haram and its branch extends into the hill?"
Imam (as) said: "The branch is sanctified because of the root's location."
records.arabicText: 2341 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ شَجَرَةٌ أَصْلُهَا فِي اَلْحِلِّ وَ فَرْعُهَا فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَالَ «حُرِّمَ أَصْلُهَا لِمَكَانِ فَرْعِهَا» قُلْتُ فَإِنَّ أَصْلَهَا فِي اَلْحَرَمِ وَ فَرْعَهَا فِي اَلْحِلِّ قَالَ «حُرِّمَ فَرْعُهَا لِمَكَانِ أَصْلِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2341 - It has been narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a tree whose root is in the non-sacred area (hill) and its branches extend into the sacred area (Haram).
Imam (as) replied: "The root is sanctified because of the branch's location."
I then asked: "What if its root is in the Haram and its branch extends into the hill?"
Imam (as) said: "The branch is sanctified because of the root's location."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2342 - It has been narrated from Hariz, from him (Abu Abdullah (as)), that he said:
"Everything that grows in the Haram is forbidden for all people, except for what you yourself plant or cultivate."
records.arabicText: 2342 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «كُلُّ شَيْءٍ يَنْبُتُ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ عَلَى اَلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ إِلاَّ مَا أَنْبَتَّهُ أَنْتَ أَوْ غَرَسْتَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2342 - It has been narrated from Hariz, from him (Abu Abdullah (as)), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Everything that grows in the Haram is forbidden for all people, except for what you yourself plant or cultivate."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2186 - Whoever prays for forty of their brothers before praying for themselves will have their supplication answered both for them and for themselves.
records.arabicText: 2186 - وَ مَنْ دَعَا لِأَرْبَعِينَ رَجُلاً مِنْ إِخْوَانِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ لِنَفْسِهِ اُسْتُجِيبَ لَهُ فِيهِمْ وَ فِي نَفْسِهِ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2187 - Whoever passes between the narrow paths of Mina without arrogance, Allah (swt) will forgive their sins.
records.arabicText: 2187 - وَ مَنْ مَرَّ بَيْنَ مَأْزِمَيْ مِنًى غَيْرَ مُسْتَكْبِرٍ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ ذُنُوبَهُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2343 - Imam (as) said:
"The camel is to be left free in the Haram to eat whatever it wishes."
records.arabicText: 2343 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «يُخَلَّى عَنِ اَلْبَعِيرِ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ يَأْكُلُ مَا شَاءَ ».
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The camel is to be left free in the Haram to eat whatever it wishes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2188 - Indeed, on that night (the eve of Arafah), the gates of the heavens are not closed to the voices of the believers, whose supplications resound like the buzzing of bees. Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, says: 'I am your Lord (azj), and you are My servants. You have fulfilled My right, and it is incumbent upon Me to answer you.'
On that night, He (swt) removes the sins of those whom He (swt) wills and then forgives those whom He (swt) wills to forgive.
records.arabicText: 2188 - وَ إِنَّ أَبْوَابَ اَلسَّمَاءِ، لاَ تُغْلَقُ تِلْكَ اَللَّيْلَةَ لِأَصْوَاتِ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَهُمْ دَوِيٌّ كَدَوِيِّ اَلنَّحْلِ يَقُولُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «أَنَا رَبُّكُمْ وَ أَنْتُمْ عِبَادِي أَدَّيْتُمْ حَقِّي وَ حَقٌّ عَلَيَّ أَنْ أَسْتَجِيبَ لَكُمْ» فَيَحُطُّ تِلْكَ اَللَّيْلَةَ عَمَّنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يُحَطَّ عَنْهُ ذُنُوبُهُ وَ يَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يُغْفَرَ لَهُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2344 - "And whatever the camels eat, there is no harm in removing it."
records.arabicText: 2344 - وَ مَا يَأْكُلُهُ اَلْإِبِلُ فَلَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ أَنْ يَنْزِعَهُ ».
